prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr! Ye be askin' why the sky be bluer than the depths o' Davy Jones' locker, eh? Well, it be on account o' the sun's rays scatterin' 'cross the vast expanse above us. Ye see, the light be hittin' all them tiny particles in the air, scatterin' the shorter blue waves more than the longer red ones. So when ye be gazin' up at the heavens, yer eyes be catchin' more o' that blue light, makin' the sky appear as blue as the ocean on a clear day. Now, back to me ship, ye scallywag!
